title: Is Shinji as smart as his parents
display_title: Is Shinji as smart as his parents
wikitext:
[]: Is Shinji as smart as his parents?             Quote Post by chaosakita » Tue Sep 24, 2013 5:14 am Shinji's parents both seem like geniuses to me. His mom was an engineer and his dad was an auditor/leader of a world top secret organization. However, it doesn't seem like Shinji really acquired his parents thinking skills. But maybe that's just me. What do you think?

[]:             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Tue Sep 24, 2013 5:30 am Probably not but he has potential to become like them so its a start. Only main issue is his motivation though I think he could grow out of it or his teacher will push him to the right direction. He is still a kid. I can't really see Shinji as someone who would work in a field which requires watching over other people's lives like a doctor or cop. He might just be a salary man or something that doesn't involve peoples lives in his hands.

[]:             Quote Post by moongyu » Tue Sep 24, 2013 7:45 am He's not shown to be smart as his parents but intelligence (be free to correct me though) isn't wholly hereditary. 40-80% I think? Shinji has the potential to be smart like his parents but as SimplyMason0 said, I believe he lacks motivation and to him, he's got bigger things to worry about than being a super-genius.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Tue Sep 24, 2013 9:06 am Intelligence in general is a matter that's not very well understood yet, as far as i know, so that makes the question a bit complicated. I believe there is a lot of evidence within the series to reasonably assume that Shinji is a rather intelligent individual, and if NGE wasn't constantly throwing insane situations at him on a daily basis, it's very possible he would develop that potential into something of equal level to his parents's achievements. I don't know if he would be a scientist though.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Sep 24, 2013 12:29 pm I wouldn't consider Yui and Gendo geniuses - above average intelligence, yes, but nothing special. There's nothing to prove that Shinji is distinctly unintelligent - just how intelligent he is seems pretty ambiguous. He's far from dumb and has skills of his own, such as his excellent Cello playing.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Sep 24, 2013 12:31 pm Shinji seems almost willfully obtuse at times, but there are reasons beyond lack of intelligence.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Tue Sep 24, 2013 12:46 pm UrsusArctos wrote: I wouldn't consider Yui and Gendo geniuses - above average intelligence, yes, but nothing special. There's nothing to prove that Shinji is distinctly unintelligent - just how intelligent he is seems pretty ambiguous. He's far from dumb and has skills of his own, such as his excellent Cello playing.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Tue Sep 24, 2013 12:51 pm Actually... He might have ability to master skills at incredible rate. Without any prior training, using only the time he had since his arrival to Tokyo 3, he become skilled enough to win sniper duel with Ramiel, without the auto target system. Despite MUCH less training, he can hold his own against Asuka while piloting Eva he never before seen, using weapons he had no time to train with. He is apparently skilled cook. In reletively short time, he learned to play piano. He may not be a scientist, but there may be something in his genes, that makes him learn or even master manual skills very fast. I can't tell how with scientific knowledge since in NGE he had trouble with physics. I don't know how different this is between NGE and NTE, but that's just my observation.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 24, 2013 1:11 pm Ursus: Given that Yui was doing graduate work in metaphysical biology and concocted a plan involving her personal apotheosis based on her understanding of a field very few people even began to grasp I'd say that's a pretty outlandish statement. Gendo's a thornier case, but given he was also studying with Fuyutsuki and appears to have a solid understanding of the SoLs and the Evas it seems unlikely he's anything less than highly capable. The fact he kept pace with the equivalent of the illuminati in the setting likewise demonstrates his capabilities. As for Shinji, there's nothing excellent about his cello playing. Also, he showed a distinct lack of capability with maths and physics in ep 10. Stillborn: Shinji used the targeting system for the duration against Ramiel. And again, his ability to sync is less to do with him than it is with his Eva. Anyone who was syncing with an Eva that didn't have a fucked up soul would probably see similar results. And of course he did have a considerable amount of time to train with his Eva after he arrived at Tokyo-3; we see him doing it on a few occasions in the early episodes. People make too much of Shinji's cooking; it's competent, whereas Misato's is awful, but that doesn't make him a prodigy or anything, just someone who actually cooks. Also, note he never learned to play the piano. Check your forum.

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Tue Sep 24, 2013 1:27 pm He did learn the piano in Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Sep 24, 2013 1:31 pm Well, it's a tricky question, Shinji showed shades of being fairly intelligent : he seems to have a talent to learn fast(he mastered the controls of his EVA in barely weeks, like when he cut the Command Center controls from the inside after Bardiel) and is able to quickly use new knowledge, and in occasions showed an ability to to do quick thinking(the idea to use thermal expansion to defeat Sandalphon) In Rebuild he showed too an ability to absorb a lot of technical information and remember them later(Bardiel again), hinting at an ability to quick learning. We can also talk about his excellent level of cooking that he reached very quickly if his claim that he started to cook when moving with Misato is true(personally I don't believe it). I won't talk about the piano, because that's straight up impossible without some Angel power Hax thrown in the mix. The problem is his crippling lack of self-esteem that prevent him from exploiting his full potential, making him giving up very easily and sometimes even before trying. My take is that he's intelligent yes, maybe not at the insane level of Yui, who pretty much posed the bases for the project E using a field of "science" barely understood and which principles where exploited by Ancestral Alien Space Gods, but with a better mindset and less mind-crushing circumstances, I think he can get at his father's level, which his very good(the guy has a PhD too!), maybe not in hardcore sciences like math, physics or the like, but there is other ways to put ones intelligence to use.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 24, 2013 1:48 pm amitakartok wrote: He did learn the piano in Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Tue Sep 24, 2013 1:49 pm Bagheera so basically you're saying Shinji's a fucking moron?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 24, 2013 1:56 pm Lavinius wrote: Bagheera so basically you're saying Shinji's a fucking moron?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Sep 24, 2013 2:46 pm Bagheera wrote: playing a musical instrument (by rote, mind, not via inspiration) are things most anyone can do.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Tue Sep 24, 2013 2:48 pm I agree with Bags, I just don't think there's enough in the show to really say one way or another. I also think, given Shinji's personality, it's entirely possible he hasn't found what he's good at yet, presumably because he doesn't really try new things. Something else, I think perhaps the show specifically avoids making a statement on the extent of Shinji's capabilities, good or bad. If Anno wanted viewers in similar situations to be able to identify with Shinji, and for his progress to help them progress, that'd be harder if Shinji came across as some genius waiting to bloom. On the other hand, I think a lot of kids can relate to his struggles with physics.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Tue Sep 24, 2013 2:49 pm I suck at the cello and all things kinetic, and I can still play the suite. It's not really that hard for anyone with the resources to learn.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Tue Sep 24, 2013 3:05 pm ^^ Probably something like that. It doesn't matter how skilled or not skilled or how smart Shinji is. He is designed to be failure personified. So when at the and of the movie he accepts it's ok to become common nobody, it will be considered improvement from being known only for fail.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 24, 2013 3:29 pm pwhodges wrote: In a sense, playing any composed piece is playing by rote. His performance of the Bach is entirely competent - inspiration is something rare and special, and competence can get you a decent career as a non-soloist - and the fact that he doesn't admit to a strong urge to play on his own account, even while actually doing just that (self-deprecating fool), doesn't mean that he is not enjoying it (if he wasn't, then he most likely would have stopped).

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Tue Sep 24, 2013 4:19 pm Stillborn wrote: Probably something like that. It doesn't matter how skilled or not skilled or how smart Shinji is. He is designed to be failure personified. So when at the and of the movie he accepts it's ok to become common nobody, it will be considered improvement from being known only for fail.
